overview: Cradle Control evolved from the traditional Elves aggro-combo deck into a resilient control archetype in Legacy. It leverages Gaea's Cradle and Natural Order alongside Modern Horizons 3 additions like Wight of the Reliquary and Springheart Nantuko to generate massive mana and token value. The deck aims to outlast opponents in attrition wars before closing the game with Craterhoof Behemoth or Atraxa, Grand Unifier.

keep_criteria: Access to Once Upon a Time and Elvish Reclaimer on turn 1
Mana acceleration on turn 1 and Wight of the Reliquary on turn 2
Threat of Natural Order as early as turn 3
mulligan_criteria: Too much mana and not enough action
Vulnerability to Wasteland or Blood Moon without early protection

examples.reason: Provides turn 1 Reclaimer and free Once Upon a Time, setting up value or enchantment for turn 2.
Behemoth is a dead draw in hand, and the hand is vulnerable to land destruction.
Too much mana, not enough action.
Risky due to Behemoth, but has ideal early curve and Natural Order threat.

early_game: Play 1-mana dorks and Elvish Reclaimer to establish board presence and mana
Use Once Upon a Time for consistency and card advantage
Search for lands with Wight of the Reliquary and Elvish Reclaimer
mid_game: Enchant Elvish Reclaimer with Springheart Nantuko to generate insect tokens
Tutor for answers like Grist, the Hunger Tide or Endurance using Green Sun's Zenith
Set up Gaea's Cradle and activate Lair of the Hydra for extra mana
late_game: Cast Natural Order into Craterhoof Behemoth or Atraxa, Grand Unifier
Copy the enchanted creature to create massive value
Win attrition wars against blue tempo and combo decks

key_cards.name: Gaea's Cradle
key_cards.notes: Core namesake that converts creature power into mana, enabling big spells.

key_cards.name: Natural Order
key_cards.notes: Searches for Craterhoof Behemoth or Atraxa, Grand Unifier to close games.

key_cards.name: Wight of the Reliquary
key_cards.notes: Replaces Fiend Artisan, searches lands while applying pressure with Vigilance.

key_cards.name: Elvish Reclaimer
key_cards.notes: Provides redundancy for Wight's game plan and can be enchanted by Springheart Nantuko.

key_cards.name: Springheart Nantuko
key_cards.notes: Creates insect tokens from fetch lands and Wight sacrifices to fuel Cradle and Behemoth.

key_cards.name: Once Upon a Time
key_cards.notes: Provides extra consistency and can be played without mana cost in certain hands.

key_cards.name: Endurance
key_cards.notes: Counters graveyard interactions and provides a resilient body against Delver/Channeler.

key_cards.name: Talon Gates of Madara
key_cards.notes: Replaces Sejiri Steppe, enters untapped and protects from global removal.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing Atraxa, Grand Unifier as fast as possible against Red Stompy
Avoid over-relying on mana dorks when facing Orcish Bowmasters
Use Gaea's Cradle mana to activate Lair of the Hydra for powerful attacks
Tutor for specific answers with Wight, Reclaimer, and Zenith before committing to win conditions

matchups.advice: Increase resilience against reanimation and add answers for Psychic Frog.
Add removal after game 1 to handle Psychic Frog and win attrition wars.
Play Atraxa quickly and use Snuff Out for Broadside Bombardiers; be wary of Blood Moon and Fury.
Add more creature removal post-side and race to get Atraxa into play.
Side in Collector Ouphe and Force of Vigor to counter their game plan; avoid Vexing Bauble interactions.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: What in the past was a very popular aggro combo deck turned into one of the most important control decks in this new Legacy meta.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: boards in which either Craterhoof Behemoth or Atraxa, Grand Unifier ... win you the game
This seemingly innocent Monk creates a mighty hoard of Insects from thin air

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: The idea is to put Atraxa, Grand Unifier in play as fast as possible
Your mana dorks are vulnerable to Orcish Bowmasters, so don't rely too much on them

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Orcs ... won their war against the Elves because they attacked on two fronts
Psychic Frog is still a threat, and you'll need to add a few removals after game 1 to deal with it
